saints.name: Saint Seraphim of Sarov

biography.short: A Russian elder remembered for prayer, humility, joy, and the counsel to acquire the Spirit of peace.
long: Saint Seraphim of Sarov lived as a monk, hermit, and spiritual father whose hidden prayer became a source of consolation for many.
His life was marked by ascetic struggle, silence, mercy toward visitors, and a radiant Paschal joy.
He taught that the purpose of Christian life is the acquisition of the Holy Spirit, and that peace of heart bears fruit for others.

saints.quote: Acquire the Spirit of peace, and thousands around you will be saved.

saints.name: Saint Mary of Egypt

biography.short: A witness of repentance, desert struggle, mercy, and hope beyond despair.
long: Saint Mary of Egypt is remembered as a profound icon of repentance and the healing mercy of God.
After a dramatic conversion in Jerusalem, she withdrew into the desert, where repentance became a lifetime of prayer.
Her life is read in Great Lent as a reminder that no wound is beyond the compassion of Christ.

saints.quote: I am a sinful woman, but I flee to the mercy of God.

saints.name: Saint John Chrysostom

biography.short: A bishop and teacher known for Scripture, liturgy, almsgiving, and pastoral courage.
long: Saint John Chrysostom served as Archbishop of Constantinople and became one of the Church's great preachers.
His teaching joined careful interpretation of Scripture with urgent concern for the poor and the life of the faithful.
He endured exile with courage, leaving the Church a legacy of homilies, letters, and liturgical memory.

saints.quote: If you cannot find Christ in the beggar at the church door, you will not find Him in the chalice.

saints.name: Saint Nina of Georgia

biography.short: Equal-to-the-Apostles, remembered for bringing the Christian faith to Georgia.
long: Saint Nina is honored as Equal-to-the-Apostles for her missionary witness among the people of Georgia.
With humility, prayer, and a cross woven from vine branches, she bore witness to Christ in a royal court and among ordinary people.
Her memory is tied to the conversion of Georgia and the quiet strength of faithful obedience.

saints.quote: The power of Christ is made known through humility and patient witness.

saints.name: Saint Nicholas of Myra

biography.short: A bishop remembered for pastoral care, defense of the faith, and generous mercy.
long: Saint Nicholas served as bishop of Myra and became beloved throughout the Church for mercy joined to courage.
He is remembered for defending the vulnerable, helping the poor in secret, and guarding the faith of the Church.
His life teaches that true authority is exercised as protection, generosity, and steadfast love.

saints.quote: The hands that give quietly bear witness to the mercy of God.
